base.heading: Navbar border top
base.subHeading: Navigation with a bar and border on the top
base.text: The navbar allows you to give the user quick access to top level pages

borderBottom.heading: Navbar border bottom
borderBottom.subHeading: Navigation with a bar and border on the bottom
borderBottom.text: The navbar allows you to give the user quick access to top level pages

minimal.heading: Minimal navbar
minimal.subHeading: Navbar without expanded menu or item icons
minimal.text: Renders the navbar in its simplest form — logo and text links only, with no expandable content drawer.

with-active-item.heading: Navbar with active and ancestor states
with-active-item.subHeading: Active and ancestor items highlighted in the navigation bar
with-active-item.text: Demonstrates the active-item underline animation triggered when an item has item-active or item-ancestor set, indicating the current page trail.

with-sidebar.heading: Navbar with sidebar trigger
with-sidebar.subHeading: Toggle button wired to open a sidebar
with-sidebar.text: When the sidebar parameter is provided, the mobile menu toggle uses data-js-toggle-trigger to open a named sidebar instead of the default inline drawer.
